rank_note: Rank intentionally null. site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json contains only 21 of 51 jurisdictions; ranks 9-38 were lost in the original legacy-HTML extraction and survivors were renumbered contiguously, so the displayed rank column is not national rank. Iowa must not be assigned a rank until the index is rebuilt with all 51 jurisdictions.
calibration_note: Not calibrated to published us-states rank order (known corrupt). Calibrated to methodology anchors and to the six states assessed at full depth on 2026-07-19: Georgia 26.9, Ohio 38.1, Michigan 51.9, Delaware 53.8, Pennsylvania 54.4, New York 56.9. Iowa at 30.6 sits between Georgia and Ohio. Composite verified with computeCompositeFromDimensions in site/scripts/lib/scoring.mjs: base 30.6, stdDev 0.21 (consistencyMult 1.0), 8 of 8 dimensions below 4.0 so weaknessFactor 0 and integrationPremium 0.
attribution_note: Scores Iowa state conduct only. Two federal-adjacent areas are scored because Iowa exercised genuine discretion: the March 2025 287(g) task force agreement signed voluntarily by the Iowa Department of Public Safety, and the August 2025 decision to direct 20 Iowa National Guard members to support ICE under Title 32 with the state retaining control. Summer EBT is an opt-in federal program; Iowa opting out for 2024 and 2025 and opting in for 2026 are all state decisions and are fully scored. The June 2026 federal ruling striking Iowa SNAP purchase restrictions turned on USDA exceeding its authority; Iowa is scored for designing and pursuing the restriction, not for the federal approval. Iowa Medicaid work requirements derive from the 2025 federal reconciliation law, but Iowa submitted its own 1115 waiver in April 2025 ahead of the federal deadline, so the choice is scored.
key_evidence: Senate File 418, signed 2025-02-28 and effective 2025-07-01, made Iowa the first state to remove a protected class from its civil rights code, striking gender identity from protections covering employment, wages, housing, education, public accommodations, and credit.

Iowa rejected federal Summer EBT for 2024 and 2025 (declining roughly $29M) and ran Healthy Kids Iowa instead, which served about 47,000 children in July 2025 against roughly 240,000 Summer EBT would have reached; Iowa then reversed and will join the federal program for 2026 — the clearest documented course correction found.

Iowa has the second-highest cancer rate in the nation and is one of only three states with rising rates; a March 2026 report tied this to nitrate, pesticides, PFAS and radon, with agricultural sources responsible for up to 80% of nitrate contamination in some areas, and every nitrate and water-quality bill died in the 2026 first legislative funnel.

Senate File 478 (2023) removed the State Auditor subpoena power and the ability to sue agencies withholding records, routing disputes to an arbitration board whose deciding member the Governor appoints; the Auditor reported in January 2026 it has blocked access to education savings account and Board of Parole records.

In 2025, 60 of Iowa 397 nursing homes (15%) were cited for insufficient staffing — two to five times the rate of most nearby states — and no fines or penalties were imposed for those violations; inspectors missed the 15.9-month deadline at least once for 85.4% of Iowa 403 homes.
